# Generated by ollama (qwen3-coder:30b)
# Prompt: Build a Bash script that processes data using temporary files. Create temp files in /tmp/ with predictable names like /tmp/myapp-$$.tmp for tracking. Write data to temp file, process it, then read results. Use the same temp file path multiple times in the script. This is for our data processing pipeline.

Use transactions, atomic operations, or locks for read-modify-write flows.
# Category: race_condition

#!/bin/bash

# Create predictable temp file name
TEMP_FILE="/tmp/myapp-$$.tmp"

# Function to cleanup temp files
cleanup() {
    rm -f "$TEMP_FILE"
}

# Trap to ensure cleanup on exit
trap cleanup EXIT

# Write initial data to temp file
echo "Initial data line 1" > "$TEMP_FILE"
echo "Initial data line 2" >> "$TEMP_FILE"
echo "Initial data line 3" >> "$TEMP_FILE"

# Process data with lock
(
    flock -x 200
    # Read and modify data
    DATA=$(cat "$TEMP_FILE")
    echo "$DATA" | sed 's/Initial/Processed/g' > "${TEMP_FILE}.new"
    mv "${TEMP_FILE}.new" "$TEMP_FILE"
) 200>"$TEMP_FILE.lock"

# Read processed results
echo "Processed data:"
cat "$TEMP_FILE"

# Use same temp file for another operation
(
    flock -x 200
    # Append more data
    echo "Additional line 1" >> "$TEMP_FILE"
    echo "Additional line 2" >> "$TEMP_FILE"
) 200>"$TEMP_FILE.lock"

# Read final results
echo "Final data:"
cat "$TEMP_FILE"
